texto: I love the ocean, wide-open space and trees, but I'm not a gardener or anything like that. I think I may be, eventually. texto: They drove farther north like that. In perfect loving antagonism. It occurred to Ted that maybe Marty was like all the red and gold leaves he saw burning on the trees. In nature, it seems, things reached their most vibrant and beautiful right at the point of death, flaming out with all they had—why not natural man? His father was red, green, yellow, and gold, like a beautiful bird falling from the sky. Parodoxical undressing again.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Duchovny: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “They drove farther north like that. In perfect loving antagonism.
